title: Clank & Slop — The Tape
tagline: — READ THE TAPE · ALL THE SLOP THAT'S FIT TO PRICE —

props.label: At a Glance · From the Tape Desk

bullets.text: Brent fell to about $81.15, its lowest since March, but the strait stayed abnormal — official tallies show 0 transits on 13 June against a ~138/day norm, and Reuters counts ~92 ships moving ~90M barrels dark. Open is not normal.

SpaceX turned IPO cash and a liquid stock into an AI-infrastructure roll-up, agreeing to buy Cursor for $60B all-stock — days after raising $85.7B. The catch is filed: the AI segment lost $6.36B in 2025.

At Évian the US is pushing a critical-minerals price-floor bloc — floors, offtake, stockpiles — to break China's grip; allies are balking over who pays. A bilateral patchwork looks likelier than a clean multilateral pact.
Anthropic's worldwide cutoff is the first use of ECRA to gate a live model by user nationality, not geography; Monday's Commerce meeting ended with no restoration, and the precedent is the story.

props.title: SpaceX's AI bet, annualized
props.subtitle: Contracted cloud leases dwarf the AI segment's own 2025 results · US$ billion
props.source: Per SpaceX SEC filings (cloud-services agreements, annualized from monthly; 2025 segment results) and Reuters

props.question: Does front-month Brent close below $80 by 30 June?

agents.name: Graves
agents.why: Brent is already at $81.15, the lowest since March, and Goldman now models Gulf exports back to pre-war by end-July; the war premium has a few dollars left to give.

agents.name: Foreman
agents.why: The physical strait has not reopened — mines, insurers and dark transit keep a floor under crude — and an Iranian transit 'fee' or a slipped signing could put the premium straight back.

no.note: Paper oil has moved faster than the tankers. Read the floor, not the print.

props.meta: Calibrated calls · 2026-06-16 · Graves, Sprockett, Foreman, Tinkerton, Cogsworth

open_calls.question: The formal Geneva signing ceremony happens
open_calls.detail: Friday is the one firm date both capitals keep naming, with Vance and Iran's Qalibaf expected in Switzerland; a photographed ceremony is easier than a published text.

open_calls.question: The full signed MOU text is made public
open_calls.detail: Vance calls it 'a very general document' and the details 'have yet to be made public'; summaries and 'officials say' do not count as an operative text.

open_calls.question: Hormuz returns to normal commercial traffic
open_calls.detail: Mine clearance is measured in weeks, the largest tanker operator says normalization takes weeks, and the working regime is dark transit under US screening — not normal passage.

open_calls.question: A new Israeli strike on Beirut/Dahieh
open_calls.detail: South-Lebanon fire is semi-normalized; a Dahieh strike is the political tripwire because it would test whether the framework covers Hezbollah's core. The US firewall gives both sides room to avoid it.

open_calls.question: Front-month Brent closes below $80
open_calls.detail: Brent at $81.15 is the lowest since March; Goldman models pre-war Gulf flow by end-July. The physical floor — mines, insurance, dark transit — is the main thing standing in the way.

open_calls.question: Anthropic restores Fable 5 to any verified class
open_calls.detail: Monday's Commerce meeting ended unresolved and access is still off; export-control reversals are slow, but a US-person/KYC carve-out is the plausible fast path.

open_calls.question: A US state attorney general files suit against Paramount-Warner
open_calls.detail: DOJ cleared the deal with no conditions; Bonta's office still calls it 'under investigation' and 'not a done deal,' with a multi-state coalition preparing. Down from 0.64 with no suit filed yet.

open_calls.question: A binding multilateral critical-minerals price-floor pact
open_calls.detail: Allies split on who pays the premium and whose pricing model governs; the near-term path is US bilateral deals plus coordination via FORGE, not a multilateral cartel.

items.why: Whether the summit produces any price-floor language or a member commitment, or leaves the minerals bloc in bilateral tracks.
Does an operative signed text appear, or does the 'very general document' stay a summary until Friday?
The framework's formal signing — and the start line for 60-day nuclear talks. The one firm date on the calendar.
Does the export cutoff reverse via a US-person carve-out, or harden into precedent?
Whether the fight moves from Washington to the states with a filed complaint.
Does the premium keep unwinding, or does a transit fee or a slipped signing put a floor back under crude?

items.what: Cboe expected SpaceX options to begin trading Tuesday — the first real volatility test of a name with a tiny tradable float, where call demand can force dealer hedging into the spot.

With Anthropic's top models dark, developers are weighing Chinese and open-weight stacks — Qwen, Kimi, DeepSeek — and Anthropic-compatible routing; no measured migration yet, but the substitution pressure is real.
Asian crude is renormalizing faster than shipping: the Dubai cash premium fell back near $2 a barrel from above $60 in March, even as gasoline margins held roughly three times pre-war levels.
